name: 554 Fillmore St
description: A small venue at 554 Fillmore Street in San Francisco's Lower Haight neighborhood listed as an active music performance space on Songkick. Limited public information is available about this address as a standalone venue.

name: 715 Harrison
description: A sprawling 15,000-square-foot multi-room event and nightclub complex in SoMa featuring two dance floors, three full bars, and a state-of-the-art sound and lighting system. The venue hosts EDM and Top 40 dance parties, concerts, corporate events, and LGBT nights.

name: August Hall
description: A historic Downtown San Francisco venue originally built in the 1890s as a Victorian playhouse, August Hall hosted the world premiere of Alfred Hitchcock's Vertigo in 1958 before becoming a premier live music hall. Today it offers over 15,000 square feet of flexible event space across three unique rooms.

name: Bill Graham Civic Auditorium
description: One of San Francisco's largest and most storied indoor concert venues, built in 1915 and renamed in honor of legendary rock promoter Bill Graham in 1992. Operated by Another Planet Entertainment, it has hosted icons ranging from The Doors and Pink Floyd to Kendrick Lamar and Lady Gaga.

name: Bimbo's 365 Club
description: A beloved San Francisco landmark since 1931, Bimbo's 365 Club is an Art Deco jewel in North Beach known for its vintage glamour, plush red velvet seating, and the legendary 'Girl in the Fishbowl' illusion. Family-owned and operated for nearly a century, it has hosted everyone from Rita Hayworth to Adele and Coldplay.

name: Black Hawk
description: A legendary jazz club that operated from 1949 to 1963 at the corner of Turk and Hyde Streets in the Tenderloin, the Black Hawk was one of America's premier bebop venues where Miles Davis, Thelonious Monk, John Coltrane, and Billie Holiday all performed. The club is now demolished and commemorated by a bronze 'Lost Landmark' sidewalk plaque.

name: Boom Boom Room
description: Established in 1997 with the blessing of blues legend John Lee Hooker and named after his signature hit, the Boom Boom Room is San Francisco's premier destination for blues, boogie, funk, and soul. Located directly across from the Fillmore Auditorium, this intimate low-lit club hosts live music six nights a week.

name: Bottom of the Hill
description: Since 1991, Bottom of the Hill has been one of San Francisco's most celebrated independent rock venues and an early stomping ground for Green Day, Oasis, the Strokes, and the White Stripes. Called by Rolling Stone the best place to hear live music in San Francisco, it has championed independent artists for over three decades.

name: Brick & Mortar Music Hall
description: One of the few remaining independently owned music venues in San Francisco, Brick & Mortar Music Hall opened in 2011 in the heart of the Mission District and hosts over 300 events annually. Known for its intimate atmosphere and commitment to showcasing both emerging talent and established acts across genres.

name: Cafe Du Nord
description: Occupying the basement of the historic 1907 Swedish American Hall, Cafe Du Nord is an iconic San Francisco speakeasy-style venue with over 110 years of history and a reputation for outstanding live performances. Thoughtfully renovated in 2016, it has been recognized as one of the top 40 music venues in America by Paste Magazine.

name: California Hall
description: Built in 1912 as Das Deutsche Haus and renamed California Hall during WWI, this historic building hosted legendary 1960s rock concerts by the Grateful Dead and early Carlos Santana, as well as 1977 punk shows. The building now serves as part of the Academy of Art University campus.

name: Calle-11
description: A Latino-owned three-story nightclub on the corner of 11th and Folsom in SoMa featuring a stage, DJ booth, three full bars, lounge seating, and a rooftop restaurant. As of early 2026, Calle-11 appears to have permanently closed.

name: Castro Theatre
description: A breathtaking 1922 Spanish Baroque movie palace and San Francisco Historic Landmark, the Castro Theatre reopened in February 2026 following a $41 million renovation by Another Planet Entertainment. Now hosting both seated film screenings and standing-room concerts, it serves as the cultural heart of San Francisco's LGBTQ+ community.

name: Cat Club
description: San Francisco's premier alternative nightclub since 1988, Cat Club on Folsom Street features two dance floors, two full bars, and a diverse lineup of themed nights spanning 80s pop, goth/industrial, new wave, and darkwave. Its eclectic mix of music and inclusive atmosphere make it one of the most beloved nightlife destinations in the city.

name: Cigar Bar
description: Tucked in San Francisco's historic Jackson Square district, the Cigar Bar & Grill features live salsa bands every Friday and Saturday, Bachata DJs on Thursdays, and Mambo on the first Wednesday of each month. With low lighting, leather couches, two humidors, and four private event rooms, it sets the standard for Latin nightlife in the city.

name: Club 26 Mix
description: One of the longest-running Latino clubs in San Francisco's Mission District, Club 26 Mix features DJs spinning Latin music, reggaeton, and hip hop. A neighborhood staple known for its spacious dance floor and vibrant community atmosphere.

name: Club Fugazi
description: A historic North Beach theater built in 1913, Club Fugazi is the long-time home of San Francisco's most celebrated theatrical productions — from Beach Blanket Babylon's 17,200 performances to the current smash hit 'Dear San Francisco,' an immersive cirque show. The intimate 330-seat venue has also hosted Beat poets and historic jazz recording sessions.

name: Club Malibu
description: A lively Latin dance club on Mission Street in Bernal Heights featuring DJs spinning salsa and other Latin rhythms plus live bands Thursday through Sunday. A neighborhood fixture for Latin nightlife in the southern Mission.

name: Dahlia
description: A Black-owned boutique bar and lounge in the Mission District specializing in premium tequila and mezcal, Dahlia offers three distinct lounge spaces with Frida Kahlo-inspired decor and a nightly music program ranging from R&B and Latin to reggae and dancehall. The intimate venue hosts local artists and live music alongside its resident DJs.

name: Dawn Club
description: Opened in 2023 by the Future Bars Group in the historic Monadnock Building, the Dawn Club is a loving revival of the original 1930s–1940s jazz speakeasy that once broadcast live radio across the Pacific. The venue features house jazz ensemble Fog City Swing and a curated selection of whiskeys and craft cocktails in an atmospheric pre-war setting.

name: DecoDance Bar
description: An Art Deco-styled cocktail bar and nightlife venue on Polk Street evoking 1920s Gatsby-era glamour with live jazz on Thursdays, DJ sets spinning house, funk, and disco on weekends, and Latin nights on Sundays.

name: DNA Lounge
description: One of San Francisco's most storied late-night venues since 1985, DNA Lounge features two stages, four dance floors, six bars, and a full pizza restaurant, hosting everything from Death Guild's weekly goth night to live bands and burlesque revues.

name: El Nuevo Rodeo San Jose
description: A vibrant 18+ Latin nightclub in San Jose known as 'La Casa de las Bandas,' featuring live banda and regional Mexican acts, salsa dancing, dance lessons, and free mechanical bull rides.

name: El Toro Nightclub
address: 2470 San Bruno Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94134
description: A spacious Latin dance club in San Francisco's Portola neighborhood offering live bands, DJs, and authentic Latin music alongside a full Mexican food menu. A neighborhood staple for Latin music and dancing.

name: Encore Karaoke
address: 1550 California Street, Floor 2, San Francisco, CA 94109
description: A second-floor karaoke lounge on California Street that was a popular no-cover neighborhood spot with a large song book and a wide stage. Listed as permanently closed as of early 2026.
notes: Permanently closed per Yelp as of February 2026

name: Esta Noche
description: Founded in 1979, Esta Noche was the longest-running Latino/a gay bar in the United States, serving as a cultural anchor in the Mission District for the LGBTQ+ Latinx community with drag shows, salsa dancing, and AIDS activism until its closure in 2014.

name: F8
description: An intimate 250-capacity multi-room nightclub in SoMa established in 2012, F8 is dedicated to eclectic electronic music spanning house, techno, hip-hop, and drum and bass, and is widely considered one of San Francisco's last authentic underground clubs.

name: Felton Music Hall
description: Nestled among the redwoods of the Santa Cruz Mountains on Highway 9, Felton Music Hall is an intimate venue known for excellent sightlines, a welcoming atmosphere, and an eclectic calendar spanning indie rock, Americana, bluegrass, and comedy.

name: Golden Gate Theatre
description: A grand 1922 Beaux-Arts theater on Market Street operated by BroadwaySF, the Golden Gate Theatre is a San Francisco landmark seating nearly 2,300 guests for Broadway touring productions and major concerts.
notes: SHN Broadway theatre; also hosts concerts

name: Great American Music Hall
description: One of San Francisco's most beloved concert halls, the Great American Music Hall has been a cornerstone of the city's live music scene since 1907, hosting legendary acts across all genres in a beautifully ornate Victorian ballroom setting.

name: Halcyon
description: An early-1900s brick warehouse converted into a cutting-edge nightclub in SoMa, Halcyon operates on weekend nights with a custom Pioneer Pro Audio system, laser projectors, and programming focused on electronic, techno, and house music.

name: Hawthorn
description: A sophisticated subterranean nightclub and cocktail lounge near Union Square featuring two rooms with a state-of-the-art sound system, offering craft cocktails, live jazz on Tuesdays, and DJ nights spinning house and hip-hop Thursday through Saturday.

name: Herbst Theatre
address: 401 Van Ness Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94102
description: A historic 928-seat concert hall in the Veterans Building at the Civic Center, Herbst Theatre is home to San Francisco Performances, Philharmonia Baroque, and numerous classical and jazz organizations, and is distinguished as the site of the 1945 United Nations Charter signing.

name: High Hat
description: A cozy neighborhood bar and pizza spot on Valencia Street in the Mission with a small stage hosting live music in an intimate, low-key setting alongside New York-style pies and a solid craft beer selection.

name: HopMonk Tavern - Novato
description: A craft beer tavern in Marin County with a dedicated 225-capacity live music room called the Session Room, offering up to seven nights of live music per week ranging from rock and blues to acoustic and Americana alongside 16 beers on draft.

name: Hotel Utah
description: A San Francisco Designated Landmark built in 1908, Hotel Utah Saloon is a beloved SoMa institution offering live independent music seven nights a week in an intimate mahogany-bar setting, famous for its long-running Monday open mic and for launching the careers of local legends.

name: I-Beam
description: A legendary and now-defunct San Francisco nightclub that operated from 1977 to 1994 in Haight-Ashbury, the I-Beam began as a major gay disco and evolved into a premier live music and dance venue that hosted Duran Duran, New Order, the Pixies, and Pearl Jam before closing due to neighbor disputes.
notes: Permanently closed 1994; building demolished 2004; historic venue

name: Ivy Room
description: A women-owned, all-inclusive 200-capacity live music venue on San Pablo Avenue in Albany, the Ivy Room has been a beloved East Bay neighborhood bar since the 1940s, booking eclectic local and touring acts seven nights a week in one of the best-sounding rooms in the Bay Area.

name: Jerry Garcia Amphitheater
address: 40 John F Shelley Drive, San Francisco, CA 94134
description: An outdoor Greek-style amphitheater in McLaren Park opened in 1971 and renamed in 2005 to honor Jerry Garcia, who grew up nearby. The 1,200-capacity venue hosts free summer concerts and serves as the site of the annual Jerry Day celebration.

name: Julius' Castle
description: A San Francisco Landmark (No. 121) built in 1922 on Telegraph Hill below Coit Tower, Julius' Castle is a whimsical castle-shaped restaurant beloved by celebrities and locals for generations. It has been closed since 2008 and as of early 2026 remains under renovation with reopening anticipated.

name: Kilowatt
description: A Mission District dive bar and music venue that opened in 1994 in a former firehouse, originally home to San Francisco's Noise Pop Festival. Now under new ownership, Kilowatt has revived weekly live music and DJ nights featuring local and touring acts.

name: Local Edition
description: A subterranean cocktail lounge beneath Market Street in the historic Hearst Building, inspired by the newspaper business of the 1950s and 60s. The venue features live jazz and swing most nights with no cover charge.

name: Louise M. Davies Symphony Hall
address: 201 Van Ness Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94102
description: The permanent home of the San Francisco Symphony, this 2,743-seat concert hall opened in 1980 as part of the San Francisco War Memorial and Performing Arts Center. It is widely regarded as one of the premier symphony halls on the West Coast.

name: Love + Propaganda
description: A luxury nightclub in San Francisco's Union Square district that blended old-style elegance with a modern dance-club atmosphere, featuring crystal chandeliers, signature cocktails, and world-class DJs. The venue permanently closed as of 2025.

name: Lucita Niteclub
description: A Latin dance nightclub in San Francisco's Mission District known for its lively salsa, cumbia, reggaeton, and quebradita music. The club features DJs and dancing on Friday, Saturday, and Sunday nights until 2 AM.

name: Luggage Store Gallery
description: A non-profit multi-disciplinary arts organization founded in 1987 that hosts the longest-standing experimental music series in the Bay Area, with weekly shows since 1991. The gallery serves as an inclusive space for visual art, performance, and experimental music from emerging and established artists.

name: Madrone Art Bar
description: An art bar showcasing rotating exhibits from emerging and established artists alongside nightly live music, DJs, and themed events including Motown Mondays and Sunday open mic. The intimate venue combines specialty cocktails and a constantly changing site-specific art environment.

name: Mezzanine
description: A 12,000-square-foot multi-use live music venue and nightclub that operated in SoMa from 2003 until its closure on December 31, 2019. The venue featured three upper-level lounges overlooking the main dance floor and a 1,000-person capacity.
notes: Permanently closed December 31, 2019 after losing its lease

name: Miner Auditorium, SFJAZZ Center
description: The Robert N. Miner Auditorium is the main performance hall of the SFJAZZ Center, the first free-standing venue in the US built specifically for jazz. The scalable 350–700-seat auditorium features state-of-the-art acoustics with audience seating surrounding the performer on multiple sides.

name: Moe's Alley
description: A beloved Santa Cruz live music venue offering a full bar, outdoor patio, and eclectic programming spanning blues, reggae, salsa, Americana, and more six nights a week. A 21+ club known for its intimate atmosphere and diverse roster of local and touring artists.
notes: Active (Songkick); located in Santa Cruz, CA

name: Mona's 440 Club
description: Recognized as San Francisco's—and America's—first openly lesbian bar, Mona's 440 Club operated at 440 Broadway in North Beach from 1939 until the mid-1950s, featuring blues singers and entertainers including Gladys Bentley and giving an early platform to Johnny Mathis.
notes: Historic venue, closed mid-1950s; historical marker erected at 440 Broadway in 2018

name: Monarch
description: A two-level nightclub and bar in SoMa featuring a custom-designed Void Acoustics soundsystem in its downstairs club room, with programming predominantly house and electronic music alongside occasional live acts. Open Wednesday through Sunday nights, Monarch is one of San Francisco's consistently active dance music destinations.

name: Montalvo Arts Center
description: A historic arts center set on 175 acres in the foothills of Saratoga, featuring the 1,100-capacity outdoor Lilian Fontaine Garden Theatre and an intimate Carriage House Theatre. Montalvo presents world-class performing arts alongside visual arts, artist residencies, and community education programs.

name: Muddy Waters Coffee & Lounge
description: A long-running Mission District coffee house and community gathering space that hosts DJ nights, live music including forró, salsa, and world music, and weekly community events. As of 2025, the owners were in negotiations to sell to new ownership that planned to expand live entertainment programming.
notes: Active (Songkick); primarily a coffee shop with regular live music/DJ events

name: Music Concourse
address: Music Concourse Drive, Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, CA 94118
description: An open-air oval plaza in the heart of Golden Gate Park anchored by the Spreckels Temple of Music (Bandshell), a landmark built in 1899 that hosts free public concerts. Flanked by the de Young Museum and California Academy of Sciences, it has been a venue for public performances since 1894.
notes: Outdoor public venue in Golden Gate Park; Spreckels Bandshell is focal performance space

name: Oasis
description: A 6,000-square-foot theater, cabaret, and nightclub in SoMa owned by drag legend D'Arcy Drollinger, offering a diverse roster of drag shows, original theatrical productions, live music, and DJ dance nights. Established in 2015 in a converted gay bathhouse, Oasis is one of San Francisco's premier LGBTQ+ entertainment destinations.

name: Paname
description: A French-inspired nightclub and live music venue that opened in January 2025 at 523 Broadway in North Beach, featuring a 3D immersive Void sound system and a full-scale Parisian building façade interior. Paname blends live music on weekdays and DJ dance nights on weekends.

name: Peacock Lounge
description: A neighborhood dive bar and event space in the Lower Haight that has hosted live music and community events since 1960, with a full bar serving beer, wine, and cocktails. The Peacock Lounge regularly features experimental and jazz performers alongside private event rentals.

name: PianoFight
description: An indie entertainment venue that operated from 2014 to March 2023 in the Tenderloin, featuring two black box theaters, a cabaret stage, and a full restaurant and bar. Over its run PianoFight hosted more than 6,700 performances spanning comedy, music, theater, and drag before closing due to post-pandemic attendance challenges.

name: Pilsner Inn
description: A longtime Castro neighborhood gay bar featuring 28 beers on tap, a large back patio garden with a koi pond, pool table, and regular live music and trivia nights. Open daily until 2 AM, the Pilsner Inn has been a beloved community gathering spot in the Castro for decades.

name: Providence
description: An elegant two-level boutique nightclub on Polk Street specializing in VIP experiences and upscale celebrations, featuring a copper-topped bar, upstairs lounge, and curated cocktail list. Open on weekends for dancing and nightlife.

name: Public Works
description: Public Works is a community-minded nightclub and event space in San Francisco's Mission District, featuring a Funktion-One sound system and a sprung hardwood dance floor. Since opening in 2010, it has been a beloved home for underground electronic music, live performances, comedy, and arts events.

name: Regency Center
description: A historic 1909 Scottish Rite temple turned premier event and concert venue, featuring the 1,400-capacity Regency Ballroom with 35-foot ceilings, 22 teardrop chandeliers, and a horseshoe balcony. Its three spaces together host over 2,300 guests across 15,000 square feet.

name: Rickshaw Stop
description: A quirky, eclectic 4,000-square-foot former TV studio turned live music venue on Fell Street, known for hosting an impressive roster of rising and established artists across nearly every genre. Its intimate atmosphere, vintage decor, and balcony mezzanine make it one of San Francisco's most beloved small venues.

name: Rio Theatre
description: A beloved Santa Cruz performing arts venue established in 1949, hosting concerts, lectures, film screenings, and theatrical performances on a 40-foot-wide stage. With 685 seats and a full bar, it is a cultural anchor of the Santa Cruz community.

name: Roccapulco
description: An internationally renowned salsa club and restaurant spanning 14,000 square feet in San Francisco's Mission District, offering salsa lessons and live Latin bands and DJs Thursday through Saturday. The venue hosts up to 499 guests and is celebrated for its vibrant Latin and tropical music programming.

name: Rouge Night Club
description: Rouge Night Club & Lounge was a nightlife venue at the corner of Broadway and Polk Street in San Francisco's Polk Gulch neighborhood, featuring DJs, themed nights, and a lively bar atmosphere. The venue is listed as permanently closed as of 2025.
notes: Yelp lists as CLOSED as of July 2025

name: San Francisco Ballet
address: 301 Van Ness Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94102
description: San Francisco Ballet performs at the War Memorial Opera House, a landmark Beaux-Arts venue at 301 Van Ness Ave designed by Arthur Brown Jr. and opened in 1932. The Opera House is also home to San Francisco Opera and served as the site of the signing of the United Nations Charter in 1945.

name: San Francisco Community Music Center
description: The Bay Area's oldest community arts organization, a nonprofit music school offering high-quality instruction to people of all ages and financial means since the early 20th century. Its Mission District campus includes a concert hall in a renovated Victorian carriage house with a proscenium stage.

name: San Francisco Conservatory of Music
description: Founded in 1917, the San Francisco Conservatory of Music is the first music conservatory on the West Coast, offering undergraduate and graduate programs in classical and contemporary music. Located near the Civic Center, 90% of its concerts are free and open to the public.

name: SF Citadel
description: SF Citadel was a 7,000-square-foot BDSM and kink community space in San Francisco, formerly regarded as the largest dungeon in the Bay Area, hosting classes, events, and parties for the leather and kink communities. The venue is listed as permanently closed as of early 2026.
notes: Yelp lists as CLOSED as of March 2026

name: SFJAZZ Center
description: The SFJAZZ Center, opened in January 2013, is the first free-standing building in America built specifically for jazz performance and education. Its primary space, the Robert N. Miner Auditorium, seats up to 700 in Hayes Valley, with a smaller Joe Henderson Lab for intimate performances.

name: Sheba Piano Lounge
description: Sheba Piano Lounge is an intimate, living-room-like jazz and blues venue in San Francisco's historic Fillmore District, proudly Black-owned and women-owned, offering live music five nights a week with no cover charge. The venue pairs its eclectic music programming with an Ethiopian-inspired menu and craft cocktails.

name: Sigmund Stern Recreation Grove
address: 19th Avenue and Sloat Boulevard, San Francisco, CA 94132
description: A 33-acre eucalyptus-wooded natural amphitheater in San Francisco's Parkside District, home to the Stern Grove Festival — the nation's longest-running free outdoor music festival, held annually since 1938. The grove hosts up to 10,000 attendees and presents world-class symphony, opera, dance, and world music every summer.

name: Slate
description: A womxn-owned, Asian-owned, queer-friendly lounge in San Francisco's Mission District that offered DJ nights, live bands, karaoke, salsa dancing, and comedy shows in an inclusive atmosphere. The venue permanently closed in April 2024 due to high rent after becoming a beloved community gathering space.
notes: Closed April 2024 due to high rent

name: Sool Bar and Lounge
description: A Korean-inspired cocktail bar and karaoke venue in downtown San Francisco located between Union Square, the Financial District, and Chinatown, offering craft Korean soju cocktails, private karaoke rooms, live music, and DJs. The name comes from the Korean word for alcohol.

name: Sound of Music
description: Sound of Music was a historic punk music venue and bar in San Francisco's Tenderloin District, active from 1980 to 1987, that served as a launching pad for bands including Faith No More, Flipper, and Frightwig. The club helped define the city's alternative music scene before closing in 1987.

name: St. Mark's Lutheran Church
description: Built in 1894, St. Mark's Lutheran Church is revered by musicians and audiences as a top-tier performance space with outstanding acoustics and elevated Gothic architecture, hosting classical concerts, choral performances, and world music by leading ensembles including Voices of Music and American Bach Soloists.

name: Starlite Room
description: A legendary penthouse bar and nightclub on the 21st floor of the Beacon Grand hotel in Union Square, offering sweeping 360-degree panoramic views of San Francisco with Art Deco-inspired interiors. Reopened in March 2024 after decades as Harry Denton's Starlight Room, it features an all-vinyl soundtrack and DJ sets.

name: Stern Grove
address: 19th Avenue and Sloat Boulevard, San Francisco, CA 94132
description: Stern Grove is a 33-acre eucalyptus-wooded natural amphitheater in San Francisco's Parkside District, home to the nation's longest-running free outdoor music festival, the Stern Grove Festival, held annually since 1938. Renovated in 2005, it accommodates up to 10,000 attendees for world-class performances every summer.

name: Sunset Center
address: San Carlos Street at Ninth Avenue, Carmel-by-the-Sea, CA 93921
description: Sunset Center (Sunset Cultural Center) is Carmel-by-the-Sea's premier performing arts venue, hosting a broad range of music, dance, and theatrical productions in a 718-seat theater. It serves as the cultural heart of the Central California Coast.

name: Temple
description: Temple is a multi-level nightclub occupying 16,000 square feet in SoMa, known for its sci-fi aesthetic and top EDM talent. Opened in 2007, it became the Bay Area's highest-grossing nightclub before temporarily closing in 2024 with plans to reopen.

name: Temple Night Club
description: Temple is a multi-level SoMa nightclub spanning 16,000 square feet, featuring an immersive LED system and Void sound system. The main room focuses on EDM while the basement level LVL55 plays hip-hop and R&B.

name: The Boarding House
description: The Boarding House was a celebrated San Francisco music and comedy club that operated from 1971 to 1980 at 960 Bush Street, launching careers including Robin Williams and Steve Martin. The intimate 300-seat venue hosted major acts like Neil Young, Bob Marley, Tom Waits, and Dire Straits before the building was demolished.

name: The Catalyst
description: The Catalyst is Santa Cruz's most iconic live music venue, a multi-room club on Pacific Avenue that has hosted legends including Nirvana, Pearl Jam, and Tom Petty since 1969. The main room holds 800 with an additional 350-capacity Atrium.

name: The Chapel
description: The Chapel is a beloved live music venue housed in a 1914 mortuary in San Francisco's Mission District, featuring a stunning 40-foot arched ceiling that provides exceptional acoustics. It presents diverse programming from indie and folk to electronic and hip-hop.

name: The Endup
description: The EndUp is a legendary San Francisco nightclub opened in 1973, considered the birthplace of Hi-NRG in the 1980s and a cornerstone of the West Coast deep house scene in the 1990s. The venue is famous for its marathon weekend dance parties that run through the night and into the next day.

name: The Farm
address: Cesar Chavez Street and Potrero Avenue, San Francisco, CA
description: The Farm (also known as Crossroads Community) was a pioneering community arts space in San Francisco that operated from 1974 to 1987, combining a working farm with arts programming. In its final years it became a hub for punk music, hosting bands like MDC, Descendents, and DOA before being evicted.

name: The Fillmore
description: The Fillmore is one of the most iconic rock venues in the world, made famous in the 1960s by promoter Bill Graham who presented the Grateful Dead, Jimi Hendrix, and Jefferson Airplane there. Now operated by Live Nation, it continues to host top touring acts and is renowned for its storied poster art tradition.

name: The Freight
description: Freight & Salvage is a beloved nonprofit performing arts organization in Berkeley presenting world-class folk, roots, and traditional music since 1968. The intimate 400-seat venue is dedicated to preserving and celebrating acoustic music traditions from around the world.

name: The Grand
description: The Grand is a 10,000-square-foot nightclub in SoMa featuring two floors and five levels of elevation for maximum visibility, with programming ranging from house and techno to Top 40 and Latin nights. The venue was formerly known as GlasKat.

name: The Independent
description: One of San Francisco's premier mid-size music venues, presenting roughly 275 shows per year across all genres from its intimate 550-capacity room on Divisadero Street. Opened in 2004, it is praised for its state-of-the-art sound system and perfect sightlines.

name: The Knockout
description: The Knockout is a deep Mission neighborhood dive bar and music venue that has been a cornerstone of San Francisco's punk and garage rock scene for nearly two decades. Live music is presented nearly every night in the intimate 132-capacity space.

name: The Lab
description: The Lab is a nonprofit experimental arts organization established in 1984, housed in San Francisco's historic Redstone Labor Temple in the Mission District. It presents hundreds of artists annually across experimental music, sound art, poetry, theater, visual arts, and film.

name: The Masonic
description: The Masonic (formerly SF Masonic Auditorium) is a major mid-size concert and event venue on Nob Hill operated by Live Nation, hosting top national touring acts across rock, pop, comedy, and more. The 3,300-capacity hall sits atop California Street and is known for its grand interior.
notes: Formerly SF Masonic Auditorium; major mid-size concert venue

name: The Matrix
description: The Matrix was a seminal rock club that operated from 1965 to 1972 at 3138 Fillmore Street, co-founded by Jefferson Airplane's Marty Balin and serving as the incubator for the San Francisco Sound. The club hosted Jefferson Airplane, the Doors, Santana, and Quicksilver Messenger Service among others.

name: The Regency Ballroom
description: A stunning 1909 beaux-arts ballroom in San Francisco featuring 35-foot ceilings and 22 antique teardrop chandeliers, serving as one of the city's premier concert venues with a capacity of up to 1,400. Managed by Goldenvoice, it has hosted artists including Rosalía, Snoop Dogg, SZA, and Wu-Tang Clan.

name: The Ritz
description: The Ritz was a San Francisco music venue located at 2274 Market Street in the Castro neighborhood. Limited historical documentation is available about this venue.

name: The Royale
description: The Royale is an elegant cocktail lounge and live music venue in San Francisco featuring jazz and live entertainment in a sophisticated setting with booth seating, an upstairs mezzanine, and a wrap-around bar. It showcases jazz bands and local musical talent regularly.

name: The Saloon
description: One of San Francisco's oldest continuously operating bars, dating back to 1861 in the heart of North Beach, presenting free live blues, R&B, and soul music seven nights a week. The tiny 59-capacity dive bar is a beloved institution for authentic live music.

name: The Valencia Room
description: The Valencia Room is a Mission District nightclub and live music venue featuring state-of-the-art audio and lighting for both DJ performances and live bands. The 350-capacity space hosts live music up to seven nights a week.

name: Thee Stork Club
description: Thee Stork Club is a storied Oakland dive bar and music venue with nearly 100 years of history, relaunched in 2022 under new ownership with ties to the Mosswood Meltdown festival. The 250-capacity venue on Telegraph Avenue presents live bands, DJs, comedy, and burlesque with a self-described 'sleazy bar for weirdos' vibe.

name: Underground SF
description: Underground SF is an intimate neighborhood bar and electronic music venue on Lower Haight Street, known for its calendar of resident-led parties and underground DJ selectors focusing on techno, electro, and left-of-center house. The space also operates as an electronic music-focused coffee bar during the day.

name: War Memorial Opera House
address: 301 Van Ness Avenue, San Francisco, CA 94102
description: A magnificent 3,006-seat Beaux-Arts landmark at San Francisco's Civic Center, serving as the artistic home of the San Francisco Opera and San Francisco Ballet. Designed by Arthur Brown Jr. and opened in 1932, it is a California Historical Landmark and the site of the signing of the United Nations Charter.

name: Warfield Theatre
description: A stunning 2,454-capacity historic venue on Market Street, built as a vaudeville theater in 1922 and now one of San Francisco's top concert halls. Its ornate interior with marble columns and a grand staircase provides an intimate atmosphere for major touring rock, pop, and hip-hop acts.

name: Yerba Buena Center for the Arts
description: A multidisciplinary contemporary arts center in downtown San Francisco presenting music, dance, film, visual art, and civic programs across its campus. The centerpiece Blue Shield of California Theater seats 757 and hosts world-class performances spanning all genres and cultures.

name: Yerba Buena Gardens Festival
address: Mission Street between 3rd and 4th Streets, San Francisco, CA 94103
description: A long-running free outdoor performance series presenting music, theater, circus, dance, and children's programs in the heart of downtown San Francisco each spring through fall. The festival showcases Bay Area cultural diversity through free admission performances every week.

name: Yoshi's
description: Yoshi's is a world-renowned jazz club and Japanese restaurant in Oakland's Jack London Square, presenting top national and international jazz artists in an intimate 310-seat venue. It is widely considered one of the premier jazz venues on the West Coast.
